ogTitle: Custon vs Cygnet River: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Custon and Cygnet River.
heading: Custon vs Cygnet River — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Custon, South Australia and Cygnet River, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Custon has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Cygnet River (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Custon or Cygnet River safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Custon has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Cygnet River scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Custon scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Cygnet River scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Custon or Cygnet River?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Custon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.18% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
